## 2025-03-08 Meituan General Aptitude Test 【Project Management Intern】

![bangwu20250308194953](https://cdn.bangwu.top/img/202503082350366.webp)

The general aptitude test was roughly divided into four parts: language expression, mathematics, logical reasoning, and data analysis.

The written test itself was not too difficult. Since it was for a non-technical role, there were no algorithm problems. The online testing platform felt intimidating at first, though.

> Lessons Learned

1. Bring scratch paper for the math section—there was too much mental arithmetic this time.
2. Be very clear about what “year-over-year growth” means.
3. The formal process may require camera access, screen recording, phone mini-program monitoring, and an integrity pledge.

## 2025-03-09 Pinduoduo Algorithm Exam 【Server R&D Intern】

![bangwu20250309112526](https://cdn.bangwu.top/img/202503091125153.webp)

This was my first algorithm test. I was not well prepared, my algorithm basics were weak, and I only solved the first easy problem out of four. It made me realize how many edge cases algorithm problems can involve.

> Lessons Learned

1. Bring a phone stand to film your upper body and screen if required.
2. It is much easier to switch to a local IDE for answering and debugging.
3. I need much more practice with ACM-style algorithm problems.

## 2025-03-09 Tencent Comprehensive Assessment 【Project Management Intern】

![bangwu20250309174057](https://cdn.bangwu.top/img/202503091741562.webp)

The questions focused on workplace decisions and project-conflict scenarios. It felt more like a personality assessment, and there probably were no standard answers.

> Lessons Learned

None.

## 2024-04-23 Huawei Algorithm Exam 【General Software Development】

![huawei end](https://cdn.bangwu.top/img/168whx-48bangwu20250423194113.webp)

The algorithm questions were quite hard. The first was about finding the minimum `k` numbers, the second was a binary-tree variant, and the third was a shortest-path problem. All of them were beyond me at the time, so I submitted early.

> Lessons Learned

None. I was in a poor mood recently and not very motivated for exams.
